segments.label: Coagulation cascade
segments.value: enzymatically activated zymogens → exponential initiation/propagation → **fibrin**.

segments.label: Pharmacological targets
segments.value: **factor Xa** (joins the intrinsic and extrinsic pathways), **thrombin**, potentiation of **antithrombin**, antagonism of **vitamin K**.

entries.name: Aspirin and reversible COX1 inhibitors
entries.memo: 100 mg: **acetylates platelet COX-1 forever** (platelet has no nucleus); the endothelium repairs itself and preserves PGI2.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **aspirin** = **IRREVERSIBLE** COX1 inhibitor (Ser529 acetylation) → ↓ thromboxane (via PGH2) → ↓ platelet aggregation.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: platelet COX1.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters / Doses
segments.value: <100 mg/day (160 mg in acute MI/ischaemic stroke; 75 mg → ↓15% CV death).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: respiratory reactions from ↑ leukotrienes (angioedema, asthma/bronchospasm, urticaria).

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: ⚠️ **NO co-administration with other NSAIDs** (ibuprofen/naproxen compete at COX1 without antiplatelet effect → haemorrhage/↓ efficacy); variable resistance (platelet hyporesponsiveness).

segments.label: Reversible COX1 inhibitors
segments.value: indobufen, triflusal, naproxen (more manageable).

entries.name: P2Y12 receptor antagonists (ticlopidine, clopidogrel, prasugrel, ticagrelor)
entries.memo: They block ADP; **clopidogrel = CYP2C19 prodrug** (slow metabolizers = not protected); ticagrelor reversible.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: antagonists of the purinergic **P2Y12** receptor (binds **ADP** → perpetuates aggregation).

segments.label: Ticlopidine
segments.value: **prodrug**, **irreversible** inhibitor; 13 metabolites (half-life 36-96h); ⚠️ **MYELOTOXICITY** (neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, aplastic anaemia, purpura) → replaced.

segments.label: Clopidogrel
segments.value: **prodrug**; ⚠️ high variability (CYP2C19/CYP3A4, polymorphisms, GI absorption, compliance) → used less and less.

segments.label: Prasugrel
segments.value: 3rd generation, irreversible; simple/rapid metabolism (Cmax ~30 min), greater potency, less variability; **TRITON-TIMI 38** (↓ CV deaths vs clopidogrel); ⚠️ greater haemorrhagic risk (history of stroke/TIA, >75 years, <60 kg).

segments.label: Ticagrelor
segments.value: **REVERSIBLE**, **NOT a prodrug** (rapid onset); inhibition proportional to concentration (no peaks), more manageable; ↓ deaths/MI/stent thrombosis vs clopidogrel; ⚠️ **dyspnoea**; no antidote. Loading 180 mg, maintenance 90 mg ×2.

segments.label: Cangrelor
segments.value: IV, reversible (3-5 min).

entries.name: GP IIb/IIIa antagonists and other antiplatelets
entries.nameShort: GP IIb/IIIa antagonists and other antiplatelets
entries.memo: They block the **final common pathway** of aggregation: total blockade, IV only during angioplasty.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **GP IIb/IIIa** is the final common point of aggregation (activated by TXA2/ADP → binding of **fibrinogen** → mesh).

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: unstable angina, myocardial ischaemia; in combination with heparin/aspirin before/during cardiac procedures.

segments.label: Abciximab
segments.value: monoclonal antibody, **non-specific** binding; large molecule → ⚠️ allergies/autoimmunity, thrombocytopenia (1%); **reversible with platelet transfusion**.

segments.label: Eptifibatide/tirofiban
segments.value: smaller, more specific but less affine, short half-life, **NOT** reversible with transfusion; no thrombocytopenia. Benefits in NSTE-ACS with diabetes/↑ troponin.

segments.label: Others
segments.value: cilostazol; **dipyridamole** (antiplatelet/vasodilator, PDE5 inhibitor); **terutroban** (TXA2 antagonist); antagonists of the thrombin **PAR-1/4** receptors (experimental).

entries.name: Heparin, low-molecular-weight heparins and synthetic derivatives
entries.nameShort: Heparin, low-molecular-weight heparins and synthetic derivatives
entries.memo: They potentiate antithrombin; **unfractionated** (IIa+Xa, aPTT) vs **LMWH** (Xa only, SC). Risk: **HIT** (thrombi, not haemorrhage).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **heparin** = binds **antithrombin**, potentiating its effect (thrombin inhibition). High molecular weight → ⚠️ immune response (anaphylactic shock).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: subcutaneous, minimal renal excretion, **does NOT cross the placenta** → ⚠️ **usable in PREGNANCY**.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: DVT, pulmonary embolism, unstable angina, angioplasty, cardiopulmonary bypass.

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value:
⚠️ **thrombocytopenia (HIT, 1-2% after 4-5 days, IgG anti-heparin/PF4)**, ↑ transaminases, osteoporosis/hypercalcaemia (>6 months). **ANTIDOTE = protamine.**
• **LMWH** (enoxaparin, dalteparin, nadroparin, reviparin): lower anti-Xa activity; venous thromboembolism/ACS; SC every 12-24h; ⚠️ protamine poorly effective.
• **Synthetic derivatives** (pentasaccharides: **fondaparinux**, idraparinux, idrabiotaparinux): strong action on **Xa**; long half-life (fondaparinux 17-20h; idrabiotaparinux 120h, antidote **avidin** via biotin); ⚠️ **fondaparinux crosses the placenta** (not in pregnancy).

entries.name: Oral anticoagulants (VKA, direct factor Xa and thrombin inhibitors)
entries.memo: **Warfarin** = vitamin-K factors (II, VII, IX, X), lives by INR; the **DOACs** = fixed dose, no tests, dedicated antidotes.

segments.label: VKA (warfarin, coumarin)
segments.value: antagonists of **vitamin K** → ↓ vitamin K-dependent proteins. ⚠️ **slow** action, narrow therapeutic window/low therapeutic index → **constant monitoring (INR)**, low compliance, food/drug interactions, haemorrhagic risk 3-4%, ⚠️ **skin necrosis** (protein C/S deficiency); not for emergencies.

segments.label: Direct factor Xa inhibitors (rivaroxaban, apixaban, edoxaban)
segments.value: direct binding to the active site of Xa (independent of antithrombin), oral. **Rivaroxaban:** no effect on aggregation, bioavailability 80-100%, high protein binding (92-95%), low interaction potential, **mixed renal/faecal excretion** (useful in renal impairment); ⚠️ contraindicated in pregnancy/breastfeeding/liver disease with coagulopathy.

segments.label: Direct thrombin inhibitors (dabigatran)
segments.value: prodrug (dabigatran etexilate), selective/reversible; bioavailability 7%, **renal elimination 80%** (⚠️ assess renal function), half-life 7-17h, no cytochrome interactions; *according to the document "absence of an antidote"* (N.B. outside the lecture notes: idarucizumab now exists).

segments.label: Newest
segments.value: razaxaban/apixaban/otamixaban (anti-Xa); soluble thrombomodulin; activated protein C/drotrecogin α.

segments.label: DOAC advantages
segments.value: rapid onset, predictable effect, no dietary restrictions, few interactions → ↑ compliance.

entries.name: Fibrinolytics and the ideal antithrombotic
entries.memo: **tPA** dissolves the thrombus by activating plasmin; very narrow window (stroke ≤ 4.5h), risk of **haemorrhage**.

segments.label: Ideal antithrombotic
segments.value: 100% efficacy, no haemorrhagic complications, oral/sublingual route, no dose adjustment, no monitoring, low cost, no unexpected toxicity.

segments.label: European guidelines (1st class)
segments.value: heparin; **fondaparinux** (⚠️ contraindicated in severe CKD <20 ml/min); clopidogrel; ticagrelor (no dose reduction, max 180 mg/day); abciximab; **aspirin** (150-300 mg initial, 75-100 mg maintenance).

freetext: **Fibrinolytics** (on **already-formed** clots): **alteplase/reteplase** (serine proteases → activate plasminogen → plasmin in the presence of fibrin; acute MI, pulmonary embolism, arterial thromboses; ⚠️ **timeliness**; IV bolus); **streptokinase** (protease from β-haemolytic streptococcus; ⚠️ risk of **intracranial haemorrhage**; contraindicated in hypertension, stroke, abdominal haemorrhage, post-operative <15 days).

entries.name: Vasodilators — overview and endothelin antagonists (bosentan)
entries.memo: **Bosentan** blocks endothelin (the most potent endogenous vasoconstrictor): pulmonary hypertension; hepatotoxic.

segments.label: Principle
segments.value: they act **directly on the smooth muscle** of the vessel (not centrally). **Classes:** Ca-antagonists (dihydropyridines → file B11); endothelin antagonists (bosentan); NO donors (nitroglycerin); K⁺-ATP channel activators (minoxidil, diazoxide); hydralazine.

segments.label: Endothelin
segments.value: main regulator of vascular smooth muscle tone; synthesis stimulated by **adrenaline, angiotensin II, vasopressin, cortisol, thrombin, glucose, hypoxia** → **ET-1** gene → preproET-1 → ET-1 → **ETa/ETb** receptors → **vasoconstriction**, ↑ pressure, smooth muscle proliferation, activation of the HPA axis. Pulmonary/renal elimination.

segments.label: Bosentan
segments.value: non-peptide **non-selective ETa/ETb** antagonist; potent, orally active (50%), half-life 5h.

segments.label: Selective ETa
segments.value: sitaxentan, ambrisentan (fewer hepatic effects, fewer interactions).

entries.name: Nitrates / nitric oxides (nitroglycerin, isosorbide, nitroprusside)
entries.memo: They release **NO → ↓ preload** (veins); sublingual in acute settings, but **tachyphylaxis** → a nocturnal nitrate-free interval is needed.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: donors of **NO** (EDRF), synthesized by **NOS** (endothelial, CNS, platelets) from arginine + O₂. **Inducible NOS** (⚠️ inhibited by glucocorticoids, prolonged/nanomolar release) vs **constitutive** (unaffected by glucocorticoids, brief/picomolar release).

segments.label: Molecules
segments.value: nitroglycerin, isosorbide mononitrate/dinitrate, sodium nitroprusside.

segments.label: Haemodynamic effects
segments.value: dilation of **medium-calibre** arteries/veins and of the **coronaries**, ↓ venous return (**preload**) → ↓ metabolic O₂ demand.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: **antianginal/anti-ischaemic**; **sublingual** route (emotional/sporadic angina), **transdermal** (**Prinzmetal** angina, more accurate control).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **tachyphylaxis**, headache, hypotension, **reflex tachycardia** (compensation → locus coeruleus → catecholamines).

entries.name: K⁺-ATP channel activators and hydralazine
entries.memo: **Minoxidil** opens K-ATP channels (vasodilation); signature ADR = **hypertrichosis** → repurposed for baldness.

segments.label: K⁺-ATP channel activators
segments.value: they open ATP-dependent K⁺ channels → **hyperpolarization** (↑ membrane threshold) → smooth muscle less receptive to constriction; **they also regulate glycaemia** (interference with insulin release → use in diabetes).

segments.label: Molecules (arteriolar vasodilators)
segments.value: **diazoxide**; **minoxidil** → ⚠️ **hypertrichosis/hirsutism** (famous side effect), cardiovascular reactions. *(N.B. some antiepileptics also cause hirsutism.)*

segments.label: Hydralazine
segments.value: direct arteriolar vasodilator (further details N/A in the provided documents).

entries.name: Arrhythmias and antiarrhythmic strategies
entries.memo: Vaughan-Williams: **I sodium, II β-blockers, III potassium, IV calcium** (+ adenosine and digoxin, outside the classes).

segments.label: Arrhythmia
segments.value: non-sinus or irregular rhythm (alterations in rate/conduction); symptom = **palpitations**.

segments.label: Strategies
segments.value:
↓ **slope of phase 4** (delay of depolarization); ↑ **threshold potential** (refractory Na⁺ channels); shift of the **resting potential** toward more negative values; **prolongation of repolarization**.
• **Pacemaker tissue** (SAN/AVN): excitability sustained by **Ca²⁺** currents; Purkinje = poor pacemaker activity, rapid conduction.

entries.name: Class I — Na⁺ channel blockers (Ia, Ib, Ic)
entries.memo: **IB (lidocaine)** loves ischaemic tissue after MI; **IC (flecainide)** is proarrhythmic in the post-infarction setting (CAST trial).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: they block voltage-dependent Na⁺ channels (**open** state) → ↓ depolarization rate (phase 0)/conduction, ↑ threshold, ↓ SA automaticity, ↑ refractoriness. **State-dependence:** more active at high rates/ischaemic tissue (ectopic foci). Kinetics: **Ib fast, Ic slow, Ia intermediate**.

segments.label: Ia (quinidine, procainamide, disopyramide)
segments.value:
they also block **K⁺** → ↑ AP duration / **↑ QT**.
• *Quinidine:* moderate Na block + **vagolytic action** (⚠️ ↑ ventricular impulse → use for **supraventricular** arrhythmias; combine with β-blocker/verapamil); ADR: diarrhoea/nausea/headache, interaction with digoxin.
• *Procainamide:* no anticholinergic effect; converts AF → sinus rhythm (**WPW**); ⚠️ **SLE**, pleural effusion, tamponade.
• *Disopyramide:* marked **anticholinergic** effect (⚠️ urinary retention, dry mouth); obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

segments.label: Ib (lidocaine, mexiletine, tocainide, phenytoin)
segments.value:
↓ AP duration / **shortens QT**; fast kinetics → active in ischaemic tissue; poor atrial effects.
• *Lidocaine:* ⚠️ most used antiarrhythmic in **VENTRICULAR arrhythmias in emergencies**; half-life 20 min, hepatic metabolism; ⚠️ CNS toxicity (confusion, seizures) if systemic; can induce bradycardia/asystole.

segments.label: Ic (flecainide, propafenone, encainide, moricizine)
segments.value: potent Na blockers, **do not modify QT**; paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia/AF; **"pill in pocket"** (AF cardioversion); ⚠️ rule out ventricular arrhythmias/structural heart disease.

entries.name: Class II — β-blockers
entries.memo: The **β-blockers**: rate control in atrial fibrillation, reduced mortality after infarction.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **β1** antagonists → ↓ slope of phase 4 (↓ automaticity, ↓ O₂ demand), prolongation of repolarization/↑ refractoriness of the AV node (↓ re-entry); SA/AV block → restoration of sinus rhythm.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: nodal β1 receptor.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: supraventricular/ventricular arrhythmias **worsened by sympathetic stimulation**; often **1st line** (excellent safety profile).

segments.label: Molecules
segments.value: propranolol, atenolol, metoprolol, acebutolol, labetalol, carvedilol, nadolol, pindolol, timolol. *(ADR/properties details → file B10.)*

entries.name: Class III — K⁺ channel blockers
entries.memo: **Amiodarone**: extremely effective but toxic everywhere (thyroid, lung, cornea, skin); half-life of months.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: they block **K⁺** channels → ↓ repolarization → ↑ plateau/AP duration → ↑ refractoriness (↓ re-entry). ⚠️ ↑ plateau → **early afterdepolarizations / torsades de pointes**; **reverse use-dependence** (greater effect at low rates).

segments.label: Molecules
segments.value: **ibutilide/dofetilide** (only **atrial** arrhythmias, AF/flutter cardioversion); **sotalol** (class **II + III**: non-selective β-blocker + K⁺ block; supra- and ventricular); bretylium.

segments.label: Amiodarone
segments.value: ⚠️ **multiclass action (I+II+III+IV)**: ↑ refractoriness, blocks inactivated Na⁺, blocks α/β, blocks Ca²⁺ (↓ AV conduction, bradycardia). Recommended in **AF** to maintain sinus rhythm (especially with LV systolic dysfunction).

segments.label: Adverse Reactions (amiodarone)
segments.value: ⚠️ **dose-dependent** toxicity → pulmonary fibrosis/pneumonitis, **hypo/hyperthyroidism**, hepatic (↑ enzymes), neurological (lipophilic: neuropathy, tremors, ataxia), **corneal microdeposits**, alteration of skin colour.

entries.name: Class IV (Ca-antagonists), Adenosine and Potassium
entries.memo: **Adenosine** stops the AV node for a few seconds → interrupts **paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia**.

segments.label: Class IV (verapamil, diltiazem — non-dihydropyridines)
segments.value: they block **L-type Ca²⁺** channels in the SA/AV nodal tissue (Ca-dependent phase 0) → ↓ slope of phase 0/4, ↓ SA pacing, ↓ AV conduction (↑ ERP/PR interval). Useful in **AV node re-entry arrhythmias**; ⚠️ excessive dose → cardiac block/arrest. *(The only dihydropyridine with an effect on the SAN: isradipine.)*

segments.label: Adenosine
segments.value: stimulates the purinergic **P1** receptors (SA/AV/atria) → opening of K⁺ channels (hyperpolarization) + ↓ cAMP-induced Ca²⁺ entry. ⚠️ **1st-CHOICE drug** in **narrow-complex paroxysmal supraventricular tachyarrhythmias**; ADR: headache, skin flushing, sternal pain.

segments.label: Potassium
segments.value: **hypokalaemia** → afterdepolarizations/ectopic beats; **hyperkalaemia** → ↓ conduction velocity (fatal effects → indication for **dialysis** in CKD).

entries.name: Antineoplastics — strategies and principles
entries.memo: From chemo that 'poisons everything that grows' to **target therapy** on the tumour's specific mutation.

segments.label: Traditional strategies
segments.value: surgery, **chemotherapy**, radiotherapy, **hormone therapy** (tamoxifen in breast cancer).

segments.label: Since the late 1990s
segments.value: **Target Therapy** (molecularly targeted therapy) and **Immunotherapy**.

segments.label: Chemotherapy
segments.value:
**non-selective** action on rapidly proliferating cells → ⚠️ ADR in high-turnover tissues (**hair follicle** = alopecia; **digestive tract** = diarrhoea/vomiting/mucositis).
• **Tumour heterogeneity** (spatial and temporal, subclones) = the basis of **chemotherapy failure**.

entries.name: Target therapy and biomarkers
entries.memo: You profile the tumour by **biomarker**: no target → no drug (e.g. **imatinib on BCR-ABL** in CML).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: drugs directed against **driver molecular targets** of the tumour (oncogene addiction).

segments.label: Imatinib
segments.value: prototype, **tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKi)** for **KIT** and **PDGF-R** (→ **GIST**, survival from months to 10-15 years) and for **Bcr-Abl** (→ chronic myeloid leukaemia).

segments.label: Predictive biomarkers
segments.value: **EGFR** (mutations → anti-EGFR), **ALK** (translocations), **HER** (gene amplifications).

segments.label: Agnostic therapies
segments.value: guided by the **molecular alteration** (not by the organ) → **NTRK** fusions (present in different tumours) treated with **larotrectinib/entrectinib** (selective inhibitors). Management: **Molecular Tumor Board**.

entries.name: PARP inhibitors and synthetic lethality
entries.memo: **Synthetic lethality**: in the BRCA-mutated tumour you remove the only remaining DNA repair pathway → only it dies.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **synthetic lethality** — PARP and BRCA repair DNA via different pathways; by inhibiting PARP in cells with **HRD** (Homologous Recombination Deficiency, e.g. **BRCA1/2 mutations**) the repair is not compensated for → the cell cannot repair the DNA → **apoptosis**.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: PARP enzyme (DNA repair).

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: **maintenance therapy of ovarian cancer** after chemotherapy (survival advantage vs placebo; historical chemo = Carboplatin + Paclitaxel).

segments.label: Selectivity
segments.value: they selectively hit tumours with HRD.

entries.name: Immunotherapy (checkpoint inhibitors) and antiangiogenics
entries.memo: The **checkpoint inhibitors** release the brake (PD-1/PD-L1) on T lymphocytes; the price: **autoimmune** toxicity.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: tumour cells **overexpress PD-L1** (inhibitory checkpoint) → binds **PD-1** on the T lymphocyte → **switches it off** (↓ cytotoxic activity, immunosuppressive environment). The **checkpoint inhibitors** (monoclonal antibodies **anti-PD1**, e.g. **nivolumab**; **anti-PD-L1**) interrupt the binding → reactivate the tumoricidal action of T lymphocytes.

segments.label: Other immunotherapeutic classes
segments.value: vaccines, **CAR-T** (especially in haematological tumours), T-cell agonists.

segments.label: Antiangiogenics
segments.value: they regulate/normalize angiogenesis; ⚠️ **bidirectional link between angiogenesis and immunosuppression** → synergistic combinations (immunotherapy + antiangiogenics/target therapy/chemo).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: opportunistic conditions (interstitial pneumonias, mucositis) → corticosteroids/immunosuppressants; ⚠️ **autoimmune patients excluded** from trials.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: ⚠️ **PD-L1 is not an ideal biomarker** (inducible by IFN-γ, dynamic); response only in ~1/3 of patients (≈15% across all tumours), not predictable.
